questions.scenario: You changed a function signature and want the fastest feedback that the project still type-checks. Which command fits best?
questions.explanation: cargo check asks whether the project compiles and is usually faster than building a runnable binary.

questions.scenario: You want to see the output from the binary program in src/main.rs. Which command fits?
questions.explanation: cargo run builds and runs the binary target.

questions.scenario: You added unit tests for a word-count function and want Cargo to execute them. Which command fits?
questions.explanation: cargo test builds and runs Rust tests.

questions.scenario: Which commands are part of normal project feedback rather than project creation?
questions.explanation: fmt, clippy, and test are normal feedback commands after the project exists. cargo new creates a project.
